key_insight: A small 4B on-device VLM can be distilled from frontier cloud models to perform object-goal navigation with near-state-of-the-art accuracy while dramatically reducing inference cost.
engineering_relevance: The work shows a practical path to low-latency embodied AI on embedded GPU robots, making open-vocabulary navigation more deployable without relying on cloud inference.
method: Use Claude Sonnet 4.6 to generate scene-graph-based frontier reasoning traces for navigation.
Fine-tune Qwen3.5-4B on 500 distilled traces to transfer spatial-semantic reasoning.
Apply E-RLVR with token-generation regularization plus quantization to cut sequence length and latency for edge deployment.
evidence_signal: On HM3D OVON, the frontier SG pipeline reached 39.7% success rate, distilled Qwen3.5-4B reached 34.5%, and the final optimization reduced token-generation overhead by 72.1% and latency by 71.8% (82.8% cumulative latency reduction with quantization).
watch_item: The reported gains depend on benchmark-specific distillation traces and hardware-aware optimization, so robustness across environments and real robot setups still needs broader validation.
paper_title: LocalNav: Distilling Frontier VLMs and Embodied RL for On-Device Object Goal Navigation
